title: Readability
summary: This passage is overly dense and spans multiple technical concepts in a long sequence, reducing clarity of the implementation logic.

anchors.snippet: Parser implementuje LALR(1) gramatiku definovanú v EBNF (Rozvinutá Backusova-Naurova forma) notácii prostredníctvom knižnice Lark . Pred samotným parsovaním sa vykonáva funkcia validate_escape_sequences() , ktorá overuje lexikálnu korektnosť reťazcov a deteguje problémy ako neukončené reťazce či neplatné escape sekvencie. Po úspešnej syntaktickej analýze je vygenerovaný AST, ktorý reprezentuje hierarchickú štruktúru programu v jazyku SOL25 .

judge.rationale: The passage is split into three short, focused sentences describing parsing, pre-validation, and AST generation, so it does not violate the rule about overlong unclear sentences.
judge.reasoning_chain: Most punctuation findings are false positives because the PDF page images do not show the extracted-text spacing artefacts before commas, colons, or periods; the rendered document appears typographically normal in those places. Several content findings about missing architectural depth are valid, but some need narrowing because the document does provide at least a basic execution flow, semantic-phase structure, and one explicitly named design pattern.

title: Terminology
summary: This phrasing is technically questionable: a design pattern is not normally something used 'through inheritance' in this way, making the terminology potentially inaccurate or misleading.

judge.rationale: Although slightly awkwardly phrased, the text is still technically understandable as describing a nested validator class built using the visitor pattern and inheritance, not a clearly incorrect term usage.

title: Overall Structure
summary: The document gives only a compressed summary of phases, but lacks a clearer abstract execution-flow description of the whole program architecture and what follows what in sufficient design-philosophy detail.

judge.rationale: The document does provide an abstract overall flow—lexical, syntactic, semantic analysis leading to XML output—and later sections expand this sequence, so design philosophy is not missing.
